guide_response.verdict: SUPPLEMENT_BOUND -- three LA-relayed spot-check findings (A: VIKUNJA_TOKEN process-scope load timing; B: claude_desktop_config.json filesystem + trust expansion; C: DB-mechanism mystery resolved + LA confirms KEEP absolutization) bound as additions/clarifications to the base verdict at commit 2c6be78. Net change: 3 NEW sub-clauses (g8-ea8_n1(e-part-0), g8-ea8_n1(o), g8-ea8_n1(p)). g8-ea8_n1 now totals 16 sub-clauses (a, b, c, d, e [parts 0/1/2/3], f, g, h, i, j, k, l, m, n, o, p). One procedural-directive note bound for the EA-8 init pointer authoring (LA-prescribed first-action ordering). Base verdict sub-clauses (a) through (n) UNCHANGED. PROCEED authorization unchanged: pending init pointer authoring + LA pre-prep checklist + EA-8 spawn.

summary.trigger_context:
Lead Architect relayed three spot-check findings via Guide-#8 chat 2026-04-28 (post-base-verdict-commit at 2c6be78). Findings classified by LA: - FINDING A (HIGH; pre-flight blocker if not addressed): VIKUNJA_TOKEN exists at User-scope (length 43) but not visible in pre-existing Process-scope shells. EA-8's pre-existing VS Code Copilot terminal won't inherit. Item 4.3 would fail without process-scope load. - FINDING B (MED; verdict-bindable): claude_desktop_config.json has 5 BlarAI references; base-verdict n1(f) covers 3 (vikunja entry's command + PYTHONPATH + VIKUNJA_PASS). Two uncovered: filesystem MCP server's BlarAI argument and preferences.localAgentModeTrustedFolders. LA recommendation: EXTEND both to include devplatform (do not replace BlarAI). - FINDING C (LOW; resolves DB-mechanism mystery): Startup .lnk + .vbs inspection confirmed Vikunja v2.3.0 ignores relative `./vikunja.db` config + cwd on Windows; uses %LOCALAPPDATA%\Vikunja\ regardless. LA decision (Finding C disposition): KEEP n1(c) absolutization unchanged for: (1) vendor-doctrine alignment (Vikunja docs recommend absolute paths for production); (2) "config tells the truth" diagnostic clarity; (3) future-proofing against Vikunja v3+ behavior changes.
Plus: Finding C bonus point on Item 4.9 (.lnk Arguments rewriting) — already covered by base-verdict n1(k); no new bind needed. Plus: PYTHONPATH explicit-call-out at Item 4.4 — already explicit in base-verdict n1(f); no new bind needed.
Plus: LA-relayed procedural directive for EA-8 init pointer authoring (NOT a verdict sub-clause; binds the init pointer's first-action sequence ordering).
summary.approach:
Supplement verdict pattern (analogous to mid-stage handoff supplements in Stage 2 Guide-#5/#6 era): adds new sub-clauses + clarifications without re-authoring the base verdict file. Cumulative-ack chain after this commit: g7-ea1_n10..n13(a-h) + g7-ea7_n14(a-d) + g8-ea8_n1(a-p) [16 sub-clauses now].
Per the no-amend doctrine on committed audit artifacts, the base verdict file at 2c6be78 is NOT modified. EA-8 references both files together (base + supplement) for full Stage 4.0.v2 amendment binding.
summary.findings_disposition_overview: - FINDING A → bind as g8-ea8_n1(e-part-0) (NEW Part 0 prepended to existing n1(e); first-action requirement). HIGH priority. - FINDING B (filesystem) → bind as g8-ea8_n1(o) (NEW sub-clause; Item 4.4 filesystem expansion). MEDIUM priority. - FINDING B (trustedFolders) → bind as g8-ea8_n1(p) (NEW sub-clause; Item 4.4 trust expansion). MEDIUM priority. - FINDING C (architectural decision) → NO sub-clause change. LA confirmed KEEP n1(c) absolutization. - FINDING C (mechanism documentation) → record in §db_mechanism_la_disposition for posterity. - FINDING C bonus (Item 4.9 Arguments) → already covered by base n1(k); cross-reference provided below. - PYTHONPATH explicit at Item 4.4 → already explicit in base n1(f); cross-reference provided below. - LA init-pointer ordering directive → bind as §init_pointer_first_action_directive for Guide-#8 to honor when authoring the EA-8 init pointer (post-supplement).
summary.residual_risk_acknowledgment: Coverage estimate post-supplement: ~83% (was ~80% per audit subagent estimate; supplement closes two HIGH+MED gaps the audit had partially missed in claude_desktop_config.json scope and one fresh-shell-inheritance gap in token loading). Remaining ~17% drift coverage gap unchanged in shape: Vikunja first-startup behavior, scheduled-task XML registration edge cases, stale Claude Desktop MCP cache, la_merge_approve ad-hoc invocation, %APPDATA%\Claude\bridge-state.json staleness. These remain in-execution monitoring scope (EX-1, EX-2, EX-3) per base verdict.

db_mechanism_la_disposition.evidence_summary:
Three convergent evidence sources confirm Vikunja v2.3.0 has a builtin Windows data-dir behavior that ignores the relative `./vikunja.db` config AND ignores binary cwd, using `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Vikunja\` instead: 1. Vikunja's own config.yml.sample documents the rule explicitly (cited in base-verdict §db_location_mechanism_investigation). 2. Startup .lnk inspection: TargetPath=wscript.exe; Arguments=BlarAI/tools/vikunja/start_vikunja_hidden.vbs; WorkingDirectory=BlarAI/tools/vikunja. .vbs sets shell.CurrentDirectory to BlarAI/tools/vikunja before launching. 3. Live filesystem state: only `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Vikunja\vikunja.db` exists; neither BlarAI/tools/vikunja/vikunja.db nor devplatform/tools/vikunja/vikunja.db exists.
Therefore: relative-path config + binary cwd are BOTH irrelevant to actual SQLite resolution on this Vikunja v2.3.0/Windows installation.
db_mechanism_la_disposition.la_decision:
"KEEP absolutization (n1(c) unchanged).
Reasoning: 1. Vikunja v2.3.0 vendor doctrine: docs recommend absolute paths for production. Our cutover decision should align with vendor recommendation when cost is small. 2. 'Config tells the truth' diagnostic clarity is worth the one config edit + one verify step. The current './vikunja.db' lie is exactly what sent us into the Stage 4.0.v2 audit rabbit hole — fixing it once at cutover prevents future confusion. 3. Future-proof against Vikunja v3+ behavior changes. One-line rollback if anything goes wrong."
db_mechanism_la_disposition.implication:
Base verdict g8-ea8_n1(c) 6-step flow (Stop verify → DB sha256 capture → SQLite integrity → config absolutize → start devplatform Vikunja → DB sha256 stability) stands UNCHANGED. EA-8 executes the 6-step flow at Item 4.1 verbatim per base verdict.
The "trust-but-verify" alternative (defer config edit to Item 4.2 conditional gate) is REJECTED — config-absolutization happens unconditionally at Item 4.1 step (d).

subclause.title: VIKUNJA_TOKEN User-scope → Process-scope load as FIRST EA-8 terminal action (Finding A)
subclause.finding_quoted_verbatim: "VIKUNJA_TOKEN is set User-scope (length 43) but NOT visible in current Process-scope shells. LA's pre-existing VS Code Copilot terminal won't inherit it. Stage 4 Item 4.3 will fail without intervention. Fix options: have EA-8 run `$env:VIKUNJA_TOKEN = [Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('VIKUNJA_TOKEN', 'User')` in its first terminal command before Item 4.3. Add to Stage 4 EA-8 init pointer pre-flight checks regardless."
subclause.root_cause: Windows process-scope environment variables are inherited from the parent process at LAUNCH time. User-scope env vars set AFTER a terminal launches are not retroactively pushed into that terminal's process scope. The LA's pre-existing VS Code Copilot terminal predates the `[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable('VIKUNJA_TOKEN', '', 'User')` invocation; it has VIKUNJA_PASS (which was set User-scope at Stage 0.0.5 + persisted through the terminal's launch) but does NOT have VIKUNJA_TOKEN.
subclause.amendment:
AMENDMENT: prepend a Part 0 to g8-ea8_n1(e). Full updated n1(e) ordering:
Part 0 (FIRST EA-8 terminal action, before ALL other Stage 4 work): ```powershell # Load User-scope VIKUNJA_TOKEN into Process-scope. # Pre-existing terminals don't inherit User-scope env vars set AFTER terminal launch. $env:VIKUNJA_TOKEN = [Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('VIKUNJA_TOKEN', 'User') if ([string]::IsNullOrEmpty($env:VIKUNJA_TOKEN)) { Write-Warning "VIKUNJA_TOKEN not set in User scope. Item 4.3 will use REST user/pass fallback per g8-ea8_n1(e) Part 2." } else { Write-Host "VIKUNJA_TOKEN loaded into Process scope (length $($env:VIKUNJA_TOKEN.Length))." } ```
Part 1 (LA pre-prep checklist callout) — UNCHANGED from base verdict.
Part 2 (Auth fallback inside Item 4.3) — UNCHANGED from base verdict; remains as defense-in-depth in case Part 0 didn't run (e.g., EA-8 forgot, or User-scope was empty too).
Part 3 (REST login fallback inside Item 4.3) — UNCHANGED from base verdict; final fallback if both Part 0 and User-scope empty.
Net behavior: - If Part 0 succeeds: $env:VIKUNJA_TOKEN populated immediately; Item 4.3 reaches the Invoke-RestMethod call directly without the if-not-token guard tripping. - If Part 0 fails (User scope empty): Part 2 inside Item 4.3 attempts the same load (no-op since Part 0 already tried), then falls through to Part 3 REST user/pass login. - Both paths end with $env:VIKUNJA_TOKEN populated for the rest of Stage 4.

subclause.title: Claude Desktop filesystem MCP server — extend args to include devplatform (Finding B)
subclause.finding_quoted_verbatim: "filesystem MCP server args[2] = 'C:\\Users\\mrbla\\BlarAI' (line 56)... LA recommendation: EXPAND both to include devplatform too (so post-cutover Claude Desktop has filesystem access + trust to both repos)."
subclause.relationship_to_base_verdict_n1_f:
Base verdict n1(f) explicitly stated: "filesystem's BlarAI argument is a target-project reference (Claude Desktop should still be allowed to read/edit BlarAI source)" and left it UNCHANGED. The LA's revised recommendation supersedes this: EXPAND to include both repos (don't replace BlarAI; append devplatform).
This is architecturally aligned with Master Plan §F ("Both registered alongside in Claude Desktop config — `claude_desktop_config.json` gains a second workspace entry; doesn't replace BlarAI"). The §F recommendation has historically been interpreted as adding a second `vikunja_devplatform` MCP entry (which base n1(f) deferred as optional). The LA's Finding B disposition extends §F semantics to filesystem access + agent-mode trust as well.
subclause.amendment:
AMENDMENT to g8-ea8_n1(f) Item 4.4: add the following step AFTER the vikunja-block rewrite:
Step (extra-1): extend `mcpServers.filesystem.args` to include both repos.
Current (BlarAI-only): ```jsonc "filesystem": { "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem", "C:\\Users\\mrbla\\BlarAI"] } ```
Target (both repos): ```jsonc "filesystem": { "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem", "C:\\Users\\mrbla\\BlarAI", "C:\\Users\\mrbla\\devplatform"] } ```
Note: `@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem` accepts multiple root directories as positional args; each is exposed as a separate filesystem-access scope. Append-do-not-replace preserves Claude Desktop's existing filesystem access to BlarAI source.
Verify post-edit: ```powershell $cfg = Get-Content "$env:APPDATA\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json" -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json $cfg.mcpServers.filesystem.args ``` Expected: 4 elements: `-y`, `@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem`, the BlarAI path, the devplatform path.
subclause.rationale: Post-cutover, Claude Desktop sessions may need to inspect either repo (LA reviewing devplatform code; LA reviewing BlarAI source for context; cross-repo refactoring during Stage 6 hardening). Filesystem access to both is strictly better than to one with no incremental cost.

subclause.title: Claude Desktop trustedFolders — extend to include devplatform (Finding B)
subclause.finding_quoted_verbatim: "preferences.localAgentModeTrustedFolders = ['C:\\Users\\mrbla\\BlarAI'] (line 63)... LA recommendation: EXPAND both to include devplatform too."
subclause.amendment:
AMENDMENT to g8-ea8_n1(f) Item 4.4: add the following step AFTER n1(o):
Step (extra-2): extend `preferences.localAgentModeTrustedFolders` to include both repos.
Current (BlarAI-only): ```jsonc "preferences": { "localAgentModeTrustedFolders": ["C:\\Users\\mrbla\\BlarAI"] } ```
Target (both repos): ```jsonc "preferences": { "localAgentModeTrustedFolders": ["C:\\Users\\mrbla\\BlarAI", "C:\\Users\\mrbla\\devplatform"] } ```
Verify post-edit: ```powershell $cfg = Get-Content "$env:APPDATA\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json" -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json $cfg.preferences.localAgentModeTrustedFolders ``` Expected: 2 elements: BlarAI path, devplatform path.
subclause.rationale: Local Agent mode in Claude Desktop requires explicit folder trust to allow file operations without per-call confirmation. Without trust on devplatform, post-cutover any LA-initiated Local Agent invocation against devplatform code would be blocked or require confirmation prompts. Append-do-not-replace preserves BlarAI trust.

existing_sub_clauses_unchanged.preamble: Three findings touched on existing base-verdict sub-clauses; recording status for clarity.

unchanged.reason: LA confirmed Finding C disposition: KEEP absolutization

existing_sub_clauses_unchanged.unchanged: Base-verdict 6-step flow (Stop verify → DB sha256 → SQLite integrity → config absolutize → start devplatform Vikunja → DB sha256 stability) UNCHANGED. EA-8 executes verbatim per base verdict.

unchanged.reason: PYTHONPATH explicit-call-out already in base verdict

existing_sub_clauses_unchanged.unchanged: Finding B includes "Plus: explicitly call out PYTHONPATH rewrite at Item 4.4 (currently implicit in spec)". Base verdict n1(f) already enumerates PYTHONPATH explicitly as field #3 with concrete before/after diff. No additional amendment needed; LA finding is already addressed.

unchanged.reason: Item 4.9 Arguments rewriting already in base verdict

existing_sub_clauses_unchanged.unchanged: Finding C bonus: "spec's regex covers TargetPath + WorkingDirectory but verify Arguments also gets rewritten (Arguments is a separate property on .lnk)". Base verdict n1(k) Part 1 already includes the line `$sc.Arguments = $sc.Arguments -replace [regex]::Escape("C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI"), "C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform"`. All three .lnk properties (TargetPath, WorkingDirectory, Arguments) are rewritten. No additional amendment needed.

init_pointer_first_action_directive.preamble: LA-relayed procedural directive for Guide-#8 to honor when authoring the EA-8 init pointer (separately, post-supplement-commit). NOT a verdict sub-clause; binds the init pointer's first-action sequence ordering. Recorded here in the supplement so the directive is preserved alongside the verdict's binding artifacts.
init_pointer_first_action_directive.directive_quoted_verbatim:
"Note for the EA-8 init pointer (when you author it): the VIKUNJA_TOKEN process-scope load command should be the FIRST shell command in EA-8's session (Item 4.0.0 area), BEFORE the .gitignore checkout from rr8.
Order: (1) load VIKUNJA_TOKEN into process-scope, (2) verify it loaded, (3) git checkout -- .gitignore on devplatform, (4) verify clean tree, (5) proceed with Item 4.0 close-..."
init_pointer_first_action_directive.implementation_for_init_pointer:
The EA-8 init pointer's "first-action sequence" section (which precedes Item 4.0 of the Stage 4 XML) shall encode the following ordered steps verbatim:
Step 1 — VIKUNJA_TOKEN process-scope load (per g8-ea8_n1(e) Part 0): ```powershell $env:VIKUNJA_TOKEN = [Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('VIKUNJA_TOKEN', 'User') if ([string]::IsNullOrEmpty($env:VIKUNJA_TOKEN)) { Write-Warning "VIKUNJA_TOKEN not set in User scope. Item 4.3 will use REST user/pass fallback per g8-ea8_n1(e) Part 2." } else { Write-Host "VIKUNJA_TOKEN loaded into Process scope (length $($env:VIKUNJA_TOKEN.Length))." } ```
Step 2 — verify the load: ```powershell "VIKUNJA_TOKEN length: $($env:VIKUNJA_TOKEN.Length) (expected: 43 per LA pre-prep, or empty if REST fallback will be used)" ```
Step 3 — devplatform .gitignore cleanup (per rr8 / g7-ea7_n14(a)): ```powershell Push-Location C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform git status --porcelain # expected before: " M .gitignore" git checkout -- .gitignore Pop-Location ```
Step 4 — verify devplatform working tree clean post-checkout: ```powershell Push-Location C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform $status = git status --porcelain if ($status) { throw "devplatform working tree not clean post-checkout: `n$status" } Write-Host "devplatform working tree: clean" Pop-Location ```
Step 5 — proceed with Item 4.0 of the Stage 4 XML (close all OTHER AI surfaces; preserve EA-8's own session per pragmatic interpretation).
The LA's truncated text "(5) proceed with Item 4.0 close-..." is interpreted as "close all AI development surfaces" per the existing Item 4.0 spec scope (the canonical phrasing of Item 4.0 in 05_STAGE4_CUTOVER.xml). If LA intended a different terminal phrase, Guide-#8 will revise the init pointer per LA clarification before EA-8 spawn.
Steps 1-4 collectively form what the init pointer terms "Item 4.0.0" (the new pre-Item-4.0 first-action block). Step 5 enters the Stage 4 XML proper at Item 4.0.
init_pointer_first_action_directive.ack_to_emit_at_start_of_ea_8_iter_1_gate: EA-8 acknowledges (in addition to base-verdict acknowledgments): (j) g8-ea8_n1(e-part-0): VIKUNJA_TOKEN process-scope load is the FIRST EA-8 terminal command in the session, preceding rr8 .gitignore checkout, preceding Item 4.0. (k) g8-ea8_n1(o) + g8-ea8_n1(p): Item 4.4 expands (does NOT replace) Claude Desktop's filesystem args + trustedFolders to include both repos. (l) Init pointer Item 4.0.0 first-action sequence (5 ordered steps) per §init_pointer_first_action_directive above. (m) Cumulative-ack chain post-supplement: g7-ea1_n10..n13(a-h) + g7-ea7_n14(a-d) + g8-ea8_n1(a-p) [16 sub-clauses; previously 14].

forward_carry_unchanged_from_base.ex_items: EX-1 (Vikunja DB-path runtime verification mid-cutover), EX-2 (VIKUNJA_TOKEN auth fallback documentation), EX-3 (S4U elevation handling) UNCHANGED from base verdict §forward_carry_to_stage_4_ea_in_execution. Note: EX-2 documentation now references Part 0 success path additionally — if Part 0 loaded the token cleanly, Part 2 fallback wasn't exercised; document accordingly.
forward_carry_unchanged_from_base.stage_5_forward_items: f5_5 (gitignore vikunja.db pattern preservation), f5_6 (LocalAppData backup retention) UNCHANGED.
forward_carry_unchanged_from_base.stage_6_7_5_tickets:
TICKET F (LocalAppData doctrine documentation; partially pre-resolved), TICKET G (python.exe absolute-path migration), TICKET E expansion sub-item (iv) UNCHANGED.
No new Stage 6.7.5 tickets from this supplement — the three findings are all closed in-scope at Stage 4.

required_acknowledgments.preamble: EA-8 must acknowledge in its iter-1 comprehension gate response the FULL set of acknowledgments from base verdict + this supplement.
required_acknowledgments.ack_to_emit_at_start_of_ea_8_iter_1_gate:
Base verdict §required_acknowledgments items (a) through (i) UNCHANGED. Supplement-induced additions:
(j) g8-ea8_n1(e-part-0) verbatim per §new_sub_clauses above. VIKUNJA_TOKEN process-scope load is the FIRST EA-8 terminal command.
(k) g8-ea8_n1(o) verbatim. Claude Desktop filesystem MCP args extended to include both repos (append; do not replace).
(l) g8-ea8_n1(p) verbatim. Claude Desktop trustedFolders extended to include both repos (append; do not replace).
(m) Init pointer Item 4.0.0 first-action sequence (5 ordered steps). EA-8 executes the sequence verbatim before entering Item 4.0 of the Stage 4 XML.
(n) Cumulative-ack chain count update: g8-ea8_n1 now has 16 sub-clauses (a, b, c, d, e [parts 0/1/2/3], f, g, h, i, j, k, l, m, n, o, p). Prior count was 14.
(o) DB-mechanism architectural decision REAFFIRMED per LA Finding C disposition: KEEP n1(c) absolutization. EA-8 will NOT propose dropping the config edit at Item 4.1.

guide_response.proceed_authorization:
PROCEED authorization UNCHANGED from base verdict. Two prerequisites still pending:
1. Guide-#8 authors the Stage 4 EA-8 init pointer at `docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/4.0-EA_INITIALIZATION_INSTANCE_8_STAGE4_CUTOVER.xml`. Init pointer must encode (in addition to base-verdict requirements): - Item 4.0.0 first-action sequence (5 ordered steps per §init_pointer_first_action_directive above). - Cumulative-ack chain restated VERBATIM with all 16 g8-ea8_n1 sub-clauses. - Note that g8-ea8_n1(o) + g8-ea8_n1(p) extend (not replace) Claude Desktop filesystem + trust to both repos.
2. LA pre-prep checklist completed (UNCHANGED from base verdict).
Stage 4 EA-8 spawn happens AFTER both prerequisites complete + LA-explicit "go" signal.

next_action_for_la.step_1: Review this supplement at `docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/4.0.v2-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_8_SUPPLEMENT_TO_PREFLIGHT_AUDIT_CHECKPOINT.xml`. Particular attention: - g8-ea8_n1(e-part-0) — first-action token load semantics - §init_pointer_first_action_directive — confirm the 5-step ordering matches your relayed intent (especially Step 5's "proceed with Item 4.0 close-..." truncation interpretation).
next_action_for_la.step_2:
Per existing stop-and-wait gate, signal one of: - "approve init pointer authoring" → Guide-#8 authors `4.0-EA_INITIALIZATION_INSTANCE_8_STAGE4_CUTOVER.xml` next turn (encoding base verdict + supplement + Item 4.0.0 first-action sequence + LA pre-prep checklist + 75-ack chain + rr1-rr8 + f5_1-f5_6 + 7 hardening tickets verbatim). - "revise supplement" + change request → Guide-#8 re-authors and re-commits before init pointer. - "investigate
" → Guide-#8 defers init pointer pending further investigation.
next_action_for_la.step_3: After init pointer commit + LA pre-prep checklist completion, EA-8 spawns in fresh VS Code Copilot Opus 4.7 session. EA-8 emits iter-1 comprehension gate; Guide-#8 reviews; verdict APPROVED → EA-8 begins Item 4.0.0 → Item 4.0 → Stage 4 cutover.
next_action_for_la.pause_state_note: Fleet remains paused (UNCHANGED from base verdict). state.json sha256 byte-identical baseline preserved.
